record.title: Norla Callison, 1974-01-24, Interview #3 - MP3 Recording
record.subject: Latah County -- Idaho
record.origin: Grandfather came from Kansas and homesteaded (1888); mother was from Missouri
record.keywords: railroads colleges and universities University of Idaho horses farming clothing cooperatives homesteads literary churches schools sports holidays politics

point.title: Conversation about paeenger trains and engineers; trains verses airplanes
point.synopsis: Passenger trains and engineers. Six passenger trains a day. Whistle-stop in Bear Canyon at Bovard. Trains vs. Airplanes. Lewiston airport. Merits of different routes from Moscow to Lewiston. Greedy Lewiston businessmen.

point.title: Competition over the location of the Univeristy of Idaho between north adn south parts of the state
point.synopsis: North and South Idaho competition for University. State of Lincoln. Drunken surveyors bear way west of where they should have been when they surveyed north Idaho.

point.title: 1893 Depression; mules verses horses
point.synopsis: Depression; poorer than Job's turkey. 1893 Wet havest. Mules vs. horses. Mules could walk a horse to death. They sere even-gaited and light-footed.

point.title: Death of the small farm; prices; advent of income tax
point.synopsis: Death of the small farm. Early clothing prices, mail-order shoes. Advent of income tax.

point.title: Early co-ops and their problems
point.synopsis: Early co-ops. Most went belly-up. Problem of embezzlement in small co-ops.

point.title: Early homesteaders; locals
point.synopsis: Hermits and oddballs. Most homesteaders were bachelors. Neighbors. Homesteaders came looking for the rainbow. Field corn bloats up a too-eager eater.

point.title: Literaries; gatherings in school and church
point.synopsis: Literary, good times. Methodists steal the community church. Gatherings in church and school.

point.title: Politics; Fourth of July events; baseball games and fights afterward
point.synopsis: Sam Callison and J.I. Mitchum, fighting about politics. Fourth fo July Tug-o-Wars: Potlatch vs. the world. Balloon ascensions in Juliaetta. Fights after baseball games. J.I. Mitchum insults Sam at a Republican rally, and Sam teaches him a lesson.

point.title: Political campaigning; corruption
point.synopsis: Political campaigning, picnics and get-togethers. Burton L. French speaks in the schoolhouse. FDR made county Democrat. Corruption in politics, Nixon vs. Teapot Dome.

record.description: With Walter Benscoter Fights and local politicking. Farmer co-ops. Depressions. Desire to secede from Idaho. Neighbors. 1-24-74 1 hr RM
record.transcript: Download the PDF Index No transcript available.
record.rights: Digital format copyright: University of Idaho Library. Original Copyright: Latah County Historical Society
